name: Caching System
description: TTL-based query caching with stable key derivation, automatic invalidation on mutations, LRU-like eviction, request deduplication, and pattern-based cache clearing.

intent.what: Provides a singleton caching layer for database query results with configurable TTL, automatic invalidation, and request deduplication.
intent.why: Reduces database load, improves response times, prevents redundant concurrent queries, and ensures data freshness through automatic expiration and mutation-triggered invalidation.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: Cache key derived from query parameters via CacheKey utilities

inputs.name: options
inputs.description: Cache configuration including defaultTTL and maxEntries

outputs.type: T | null | boolean | number | CacheStats | string[]
outputs.description: Cached data (or null if expired), invalidation count, cache statistics, or list of valid keys

steps: Use QueryCache.getInstance() to get singleton instance
Use CacheKey.fromQuery() to derive stable cache keys from query parameters
Cache query results with set() and appropriate TTL
Retrieve cached data with get() (returns null if expired)
Invalidate cache after mutations using invalidatePattern() with table prefix

logic.summary: QueryCache is a singleton that stores query results with TTL-based expiration. Keys are derived stably from query parameters using JSON stringification. Automatic LRU-like eviction removes oldest entries when max capacity is reached. Request deduplication in QueryEngine prevents concurrent duplicate queries. Mutations trigger automatic cache invalidation for affected tables.

flow.detail: CacheKey.fromQuery() creates deterministic key from table, filters, sort, pagination
get() checks store for key, verifies expiration, returns data or null
If null, execute query, store result with set(), return data
If found and not expired, increment hits counter, return cached data
After mutation, invalidatePattern() removes all keys matching table prefix
When maxEntries reached, remove 20% of oldest entries by expiration time
QueryEngine.pendingQueries Map ensures concurrent identical queries execute once

rules: Default TTL is 5 minutes (300000ms); override per-query as needed
Maximum cache entries is 500; eviction removes 20% of oldest when exceeded
Cache keys must be deterministic; use CacheKey utilities for derivation
All mutations must invalidate cache unless explicitly disabled
Request deduplication only applies to concurrent identical queries within QueryEngine
Expired entries are lazily removed on access and during eviction
anti_patterns: Do not cache mutation results (only queries should be cached)
Do not use static keys for dynamic queries (use CacheKey.fromQuery)
Do not disable cache invalidation after mutations without understanding consequences
Do not store large objects in cache without considering memory impact
Do not rely on cache for critical data that must always be fresh
Do not create multiple QueryCache instances (use singleton)

functions.name: QueryCache.getInstance
functions.description: Gets or creates singleton cache instance

functions.name: get
functions.description: Retrieves cached value, returns null if expired or missing

functions.name: set
functions.signature: <T>(key: string, data: T, ttl?: number) => void

functions.name: invalidatePattern
functions.description: Removes all keys matching pattern, returns count removed

functions.name: has
functions.description: Checks if key exists and is not expired

functions.name: getStats
functions.description: Returns cache statistics (size, hits, misses, hitRate)

functions.name: CacheKey.fromQuery
functions.signature: (table: string, options?: { select?: string; filters?: unknown; orderBy?: unknown[]; limit?: number; offset?: number; range?: { from: number; to: number } }) => string
functions.description: Derives cache key from query parameters

functions.name: CacheKey.tablePrefix
functions.description: Returns prefix pattern for table-based invalidation

functions.name: CacheKey.forTable
functions.description: Creates key for specific table or record

when_to_use: When caching database query results to reduce database load
When implementing request deduplication for concurrent identical queries
When needing automatic cache invalidation after data mutations
When monitoring cache performance for optimization
When deriving stable cache keys from dynamic query parameters
when_not_to_use: When caching user-specific data that varies per session (use user-scoped keys)
When data must always be real-time (disable cache with useCache: false)
When storing sensitive data that should not persist in memory
When cache size exceeds available memory (reduce maxEntries)
reasoning_steps: Determine if query result is cacheable (read-only, not user-specific)
Choose appropriate TTL based on data freshness requirements
Use CacheKey.fromQuery() for deterministic key generation
Enable cache by default; disable only when real-time data is required
Ensure mutations invalidate related cache automatically
Monitor hit rate to optimize TTL and cache size settings
Use invalidatePattern() for targeted cache clearing after mutations
